title: Silky Sick-Day Citrus Stir-fry
description: This egg-free friendly, citrusy and silky dish features bay leaf to provide a unique twist on traditional Kenyan cuisine for those feeling under the weather.

ingredients.name: 1 red bell pepper, thinly sliced
ingredients.notes: about the size of a thumb

ingredients.name: 2 cups of shredded coconut (unsweetened)
ingredients.notes: or use any other type of coconut you prefer

ingredients.name: Juice of 1 lime and 1 orange
ingredients.notes: freshly squeezed for extra citrusy flavor

instructions: In a wok or large frying pan, heat the vegetable oil over medium-high heat.
Add the finely chopped onion and minced garlic. Sauté until the onions turn translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Next, add the thinly sliced red bell pepper and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes until slightly softened but still crisp.
Fold in the shredded coconut and gently stir to combine with the vegetables and garlic mixture.
Add the freshly squeezed lime and orange juices to the pan. Stir well to coat all ingredients evenly.
Gently toss in the bay leaves for a moment, just enough to infuse their flavor into the dish without overcooking them.
Season with salt to taste if needed.
Serve hot as a snack or light meal accompaniment.
